attacking_style: Sprinter who contests field sprints rather than initiating attacks. Lacks the typical launch pattern of pure sprinters, suggesting positioning or timing vulnerabilities in finale.
team_tactics: Teams have experimented with his leadout hierarchy, sometimes protecting him (Trek sacrificing Venturini) and sometimes using him as a secondary option, indicating unclear sprint leadership status.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where he can survive to the finale. Struggles identified during mountainous Grand Tour stages based on difficult Vuelta campaign reference.
vulnerability: Inconsistent sprint results despite team support suggests issues with positioning, timing, or power in final 200m. Multi-week Grand Tours appear to diminish his competitiveness.
key_patterns: Used as variable sprint option rather than clear protected leader across different teams (2 instances)
Shows capability for podium finishes but lacks consistent winning conversion rate (2 instances)
Grand Tour participation yields difficult campaigns with isolated good results rather than sustained performance
form_trajectory: unknown: limited recent data with 2019 showing experimental team role and 2017 showing isolated success amid struggles
